name: USB Host Interface
description: The USB Host interface on the ESP32-S3, ESP32-S2 and ESP32-P4 is used to connect to USB peripheral devices. Multiple devices may be configured, but only one can be connected at any time. By default the device must be directly connected to the ESP32, but this can be changed by setting the `enable_hubs` option to `true`.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: An id to assign to the device.

config_entries.label: Devices
config_entries.description: A list of devices to configure.

config_entries.label: Enable Hubs
config_entries.description: Whether to include support for hubs. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Max Packet Size
config_entries.description: Maximum USB packet size in bytes. Defaults to `64` (USB Full Speed). Valid option values are 64, 128, 256, 512 and 1024. Intended for use with USB-HS capable devices like the ESP32-P4.

config_entries.label: Max Transfer Requests
config_entries.description: Maximum number of concurrent USB transfer requests. Range: 1-32. Defaults to `16`. Increase this value for high-throughput devices (e.g., USB UART at 115200+ baud) if you see "All X transfer slots in use" errors.
